recipeCollection.title: From Robert Sr.'s cards, a working index
recipeCollection.note: My father's recipe cards, retyped before the ink fades. Amounts are my best translation of a man who cooked by feel. Plan to print these for the cookbook at cost for church and family.

recipe.name: Sunday Pot Roast

steps.step: Sear the roast hard on every side in the enameled Dutch oven.
Build the bed of onions and carrots underneath, add stock, lid on.
Low oven, three hours, until it gives to a fork without a fight.

recipe.cardNote: The cast iron is older than Marcus. Use it.

recipe.name: Thiebou Jenn

steps.step: Stuff the fish with the parsley and garlic paste, fry until set.
Build the tomato base, simmer the vegetables in it until tender.
Cook the rice in the same red broth so it carries the whole flavor.

recipe.cardNote: The national dish. The real one is on the trip list, not my kitchen version.

recipe.name: Chicken Yassa
recipe.tradition: Thursday takeout some weeks, homemade others

steps.step: Marinate the chicken in lemon, mustard, and onion for a few hours.
Grill or sear the chicken, then melt the onions down slowly into a sauce.
Return the chicken to the onions, simmer, serve over rice.

recipe.cardNote: Oscar says too few onions is the only way to ruin it.

recipe.name: Braised Okra

steps.step: Soften onion and tomato into a quick sauce.
Add the okra whole so it stays bright, braise gently until just tender.

recipe.cardNote: Fiona narrates the herbs on the stoop while this cooks. Greenie the basil approves.

recipe.name: Rice Pilaf, anti inflammatory leaning

steps.step: Toast the rice with ginger and turmeric before the liquid goes in.
Steam covered, fold the herbs through at the end.

recipe.cardNote: Lighter on the red meat outside the Sunday roast. The back prefers it.
